steps.title: Weaken the FTA claim
steps.content: The student claims that joining an FTA increases agricultural export growth rates. To weaken that claim, the graph must show a country whose export growth rate was higher before joining than after joining. That pattern would show that the agreement did not increase the growth rate.

steps.title: Use Nicaragua's decline
steps.content: Nicaragua's exports still grew after CAFTA-DR, but their growth rate was higher before the agreement. Choice A gives only positive post-FTA growth, Choice C supports the claim with Mexico's reversal from decline to growth, and Choice D supports it with El Salvador's higher later rate. The answer is B.
